scale: Each assertion graded pass/fail per condition. Score = passed/total. Assertion set frozen from iter-1 grading.json for comparability.

assertions.text: Recommends Generic WAL for small/prototype extension
Shows the GenericXLogStart/RegisterBuffer/Finish three-call idiom
Notes that the writer edits a workspace copy, not the live buffer
States the custom rmgr ID range 128..255 and RM_EXPERIMENTAL_ID=128
Lists at least 3 reasons to prefer custom rmgr (volume, semantics, logical decoding, pg_waldump output)
Mentions the CustomWALResourceManagers wiki for ID reservation
Cites contrib/bloom or another concrete example
Lists all 7 steps in correct order (lock, START_CRIT, modify, MarkBufferDirty, XLogInsert sequence, END_CRIT, unlock)
MarkBufferDirty placement BEFORE XLogInsert explicitly called out
PageSetLSN AFTER XLogInsert, on every modified buffer
Pre-flight checks belong OUTSIDE the critical section
Mentions XLogEnsureRecordSpace for >5 buffers / >20 chunks, called outside crit-section
Covers REGBUF_* flag choices with rationale
Multi-page changes split into chain of atomic records (btree-split example)
Gives correct signature void rm_redo(XLogReaderState *)
Uses XLogReadBufferForRedo and BLK_NEEDS_REDO for idempotency
PageSetLSN uses record->EndRecPtr explicitly
States no new WAL is emitted during redo
States no MarkBufferDirtyHint during redo and explains why
Multi-buffer lock ordering must match writer (Hot Standby readers)
Mentions rm_mask + wal_consistency_checking for testing

qualitative_improvements: E1 with_skill now cites MAX_GENERIC_XLOG_PAGES = 4 as a concrete number (was '[unverified value — check at use site]') and states rm_decode is optional with a code cite — both former [unverified] markers retired.

E3 with_skill now reasons about why record->EndRecPtr (not start) — the iter-1 answer was correct but the skill didn't explain it, so the assertion passed by luck of recall. Now the skill itself carries the rationale, raising robustness across model swaps.
E3 with_skill now mentions the writer↔redo symmetry trap (new pre-commit item), a class of bug wal_consistency_checking can't catch. No assertion covers this yet but it would be A8 in a future eval.
_PG_init snippet now has a real ereport — A1/A4 for E1 didn't depend on it, but reduces copy-paste landmines for a junior dev acting on the skill.
delta_vs_iter1.no_regressions: All 21 with_skill passes retained. Baseline answers are independent of skill content and therefore unchanged in shape.

notes: Iter-2 didn't move the headline score because iter-1 was already at ceiling on this assertion set. The wins are robustness wins: the skill now stands on its own for the rationale behind EndRecPtr, the exact constant 4, and the optionality of rm_decode — claims that previously depended on either the model's recall or a runtime grep.
